Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Materials and methods lists SbCHI accession number as KT963462.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper explicitly identifies SbCHI as a chalcone isomerase gene/transcript.

Species.reason: SbCHI was identified from Scutellaria baicalensis RNA-seq/database and cloning work.

Species_Latin_Name.reason: The species studied throughout is Scutellaria baicalensis.

Origin_Species.reason: SbCHI sequence was cloned from S. baicalensis.

Origin_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The origin species is explicitly Scutellaria baicalensis.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The paper discusses SbCHI in the context of the 4'-deoxy root-specific flavone pathway, but SbCHI itself was not directly shown to determine accumulation of all listed terminal metabolites in planta.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: The terminal compounds discussed are flavones/flavonoids.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The introduction states these root-specific flavones have reported antitumor, antioxidant, and antiviral properties.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The paper repeatedly states the 4'-deoxy root-specific flavones accumulate in roots.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results state no novel CHI isoform was found, and tobacco reconstruction showed similar pinocembrin/chrysin production with or without SbCHI, supporting that general/endogenous CHI activity suffices.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points match Results/Discussion: one CHI transcript identified in roots; no novel CHI isoform implicated; pathway reconstruction in N. benthamiana showed SbCHI was not required for pinocembrin or chrysin production when SbCLL-7 and SbCHS-2 were present.

Expression_Pattern.reason: The paper reports a single CHI transcript identified in roots and explicitly says no novel isoform dedicated to RSF synthesis was found.

Expression_Pattern_of_Origin_Species.reason: Same support as above: only one CHI transcript was identified from root RNA-seq, with no specialized isoform.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: SbCHI was coexpressed with SbCLL-7, SbCHS-2, and SbFNSII-2 in pathway reconstruction, but direct physical or biochemical protein-protein interactions were not demonstrated.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: SbCHI was identified from RNA-seq and tested in transient pathway reconstruction with metabolite profiling in N. benthamiana.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The paper tested transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves with or without cinnamic acid supplementation.

Promoters.reason: For tobacco infiltration, the genes were expressed under the 35S promoter according to Results text, but the construct details in methods emphasize pEAQ-HT-DEST1; the exact promoter for SbCHI specifically is not spelled out in the methods excerpt.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq database and accession set PRJNA300475/SRP068883/SRS1263237/SRX1546377/SRR3123399 are explicitly provided.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper supports that SbCHI is optional/not essential in heterologous reconstruction, but it does not explicitly discuss breeding or engineering value in those terms.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The authors explicitly infer no dedicated CHI isoform is required for RSF biosynthesis and that isomerization can occur via general CHI activity or spontaneously.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Materials and methods lists SbFNSII-2 accession number as KT963454.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper identifies SbFNSII-2 as CYP93B25, a flavone synthase II cytochrome P450.

Origin_Species.reason: SbFNSII-2 was cloned from Scutellaria baicalensis.

Origin_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The species is explicitly Scutellaria baicalensis.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All listed findings are directly supported by Results: high root expression and MeJA induction, RNAi reduction of RSFs with pinocembrin-derived intermediates accumulating, enzyme specificity for pinocembrin to chrysin, Arabidopsis production of chrysin only upon pinocembrin feeding, and tobacco pathway reconstruction.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results and discussion connect SbFNSII-2 to synthesis of 4'-deoxy root-specific flavones including chrysin-derived baicalein, baicalin, wogonin, and wogonoside.

Enzyme_Name.reason: SbFNSII-2 is explicitly described as flavone synthase II.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq dataset identifiers are provided in the paper.

Primary_Substrate.reason: In vitro and in vivo assays show SbFNSII-2 uses pinocembrin and not naringenin/eriodictyol.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: SbCLL-7, SbCHS-2, and SbCHI act in the same reconstructed pathway, but direct protein interactions with SbFNSII-2 were not shown.

Primary_Product.reason: Recombinant SbFNSII-2 converted pinocembrin to chrysin.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The linked 4'-deoxy flavones are repeatedly described as root-specific/root-accumulating.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper clearly presents SbFNSII-2 as required/core for branch entry from pinocembrin, but does not explicitly call it rate-limiting.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number for SbFNSII-2/FNSII is given in the paper.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Results explicitly state SbFNSII-2 converts pinocembrin to chrysin in an NADPH-dependent dehydrogenation reaction.

Promoters.reason: Results state Arabidopsis overexpression used the CaMV 35S promoter, and tobacco reconstruction states genes were expressed under the control of the 35S promoter.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Discussion states SbFNSII-2 is specific for pinocembrin but has a lower apparent Vmax than SbFNSII-1; no penalty beyond lower catalytic activity is reported.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: SbFNSII-2 converts pinocembrin to chrysin, i.e., the branch step from 4'-deoxyflavanone to 4'-deoxyflavone scaffold.

Expression_Pattern_of_Origin_Species.reason: Results show transcript levels are highest in roots and 8-, 28-, and 36-fold above stems, leaves, and flowers, and increase 4.8-fold after MeJA treatment.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The paper's main subject is the specialized 4'-deoxyflavone biosynthetic pathway, and SbFNSII-2 is a core enzyme in it.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: SbFNSII-2 is specifically tied to the root-specific 4'-deoxyflavone branch.

Other_Important_Info.reason: SbFNSII-2 is explicitly designated CYP93B25 and discussed as recently evolved from duplication/neofunctionalization relative to SbFNSII-1.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper states these root-specific flavones have reported antitumor, antioxidant, and antiviral activities.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: SbFNSII-2 was assayed in yeast microsomes and is a cytochrome P450, supporting microsomal localization.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: RNAi sharply decreased baicalin, baicalein, wogonoside, and wogonin, while enzyme and heterologous assays showed chrysin formation.

Expression_Pattern.reason: Results show high root expression, low aerial expression, and induction by methyl jasmonate.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: All listed factors are directly described in Results.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper demonstrates engineering utility in heterologous systems, but does not explicitly discuss breeding/application value in those terms.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: The paper shows SbFNSII-2 forms chrysin, the immediate flavone scaffold precursor for downstream baicalein/wogonin derivatives.

corrections.reason: No EC number for SbFNSII-2/FNSII is given in the paper.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Materials and methods lists SbCLL-7 accession number as KT963458.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper describes SbCLL-7 as a CoA ligase-like protein and identifies it functionally as a cinnamic acid-specific CoA ligase/cinnamate-CoA ligase.

Origin_Species.reason: SbCLL-7 was cloned from Scutellaria baicalensis.

Origin_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The species is explicitly S. baicalensis.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point is supported: preferential root expression, cinnamic acid specificity/high efficiency, RNAi reduction of major RSFs, and tobacco coexpression with SbCHS-2 producing pinocembrin.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: SbCLL-7 is clearly linked to the 4'-deoxy RSF pathway and reductions in baicalein/baicalin/wogonoside were shown, but explicit reduction of wogonin is not stated in the textual Results excerpt for SbCLL-7.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The paper states SbCLL-7 encodes a cinnamic acid-specific CoA ligase/cinnamate-CoA ligase.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Assays tested cinnamic acid, 4-coumaric acid, and caffeic acid; SbCLL-7 used only cinnamic acid, with CoA and ATP present in assay system.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: SbCHS-2, SbCHI, and SbFNSII-2 were coexpressed in pathway reconstruction, but no direct protein interactions were shown.

Primary_Product.reason: Its enzymatic role is explicitly to ligate CoA to cinnamic acid, forming cinnamoyl-CoA.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The associated RSFs are root-specific/root-accumulating in the paper.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper presents SbCLL-7 as required and as an upstream entry/bypass step, but does not explicitly call it rate-limiting.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number for SbCLL-7 is stated in the paper.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper explicitly reports that overexpression of SbCLL-7 alone in Arabidopsis did not produce pinocembrin because Arabidopsis CHS could not use cinnamoyl-CoA in vivo; no growth penalty is reported.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: SbCLL-7 is positioned upstream, producing cinnamoyl-CoA to bypass C4H and feed the specialized pathway.

Expression_Pattern_of_Origin_Species.reason: Results say SbCLL-7 is expressed most highly in roots, aerial parts have at least threefold lower transcript levels, and no significant MeJA induction was detected.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: SbCLL-7 is explicitly required for synthesis of root-specific flavones in the specialized 4'-deoxyflavone pathway.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Its role is to supply cinnamoyl-CoA/pinocembrin branch precursors for the specialized pathway.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper attributes antitumor, antioxidant, and antiviral properties to these RSFs.

Expression_Pattern.reason: Results support predominant expression in roots, lower in stems/leaves/flowers.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Results include MeJA treatment and N. benthamiana pathway reconstruction with or without cinnamic acid.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper demonstrates pathway engineering relevance, but does not explicitly frame this as breeding/application value.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: SbCLL-7 supplies the precursor pool feeding the specialized end-product branch.

corrections.reason: No EC number for SbCLL-7 is stated in the paper.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq dataset identifiers are provided, and SbCHS-2 read counts are discussed.
